#doc: Public viewer for a single stream. Page chrome only — no per-stream text. Splash screen of the video carries the deceased's name, date and location.

$Para1.#content: There is a row of buttons at the bottom of the video panel, one of which allows you to view the video in full screen.

$Para2.#content: If the message ‘Sorry, this media is offline’ shows at the top of the video panel, please refresh the panel — or this page — near the time the service is due to start, as the browser may not detect when the stream has gone live.

$Para3.#content: Please note: streaming is not always successful, owing to many different causes, so as well as streaming the service we separately record it. In the event of the stream failing, or if you are unable to view it for another reason, please return to this page a few hours later, when the video panel above will be replaced by a link that will allow you to view the recording.

$Para4.#content: A fully edited video will be available a few days later, to view or download. This may include some or all of the following: coverage of the arrival and procession, multiple camera angles, close-up views of speakers, original recordings of commercial music as played during the service or added afterwards, and scrolling text and photographs supplied by the family.

$RecordingPara.#content:
A fully edited tribute video will be available in a few days, to view or download at the discretion of the family. This will include some or all of the following: coverage of the arrival and departure, multiple camera angles, closer views of those speaking and original recordings of commercial music played during the service.

In the meantime, here is a link to a recording of the stream, for those who may have missed it.
